diagnosis: **: This report is being revised to correct the specimen "BS" site from left to "right" breast. The remainder of the diagnosis is unchanged. A, "RIGHT BREAST MASS", (INCISIONAL BIOPSY; INFILTRATE DUCTAL CARCINOMA WITH PROMINENT CRIBRIFORM PATTERN. TUMOR SIZE; 2 cM. B. RIGHT BREAST , (MASTECTOMY) :
margins: are inked blue. Sectioning shows recent biopsy cavity approximately 5 cm in diameter deep to the suture. On the medial aspect of this biopsy cavity, another tunor is identified. While contiguous with the cavity, the bulk of the tumor is more medial. This is felt to represent a single "dunbell" shaped tunor rather than two separate tumors. Maximal dimensions of this tumor are $x 3.5.x 3 om. This tunor is also located in the upper lateral quadrant and is 2 om from the deep resection margin. Further firm tissue suspicious for tumor is identified in the lateral aspect of the biopsy cavity and measures 2 cm. Again this is 2 om from the deep resection margin. Measuring from the medially located tumor to the lesion present at the lateral aspect of the biopsy cavity the maximum tumor diameter is 7 cm. The axilla is sampled from medial to lateral.
